invalid_review_attempts.reason: outer command succeeded but result was not JSON-only and returned markdown/prose, so it was not counted as a valid pack-level review

parsed_review.summary: CP00-168 approved. It preserves the Risk A descriptor-only/no-write boundary for 10 Master Data permission/audit fixture decision test units; customer-facing outputs remain trimmed, internal refs remain evidence-only, and handoff to CP00-169/RP04.P06.M06.S06 is valid. The single P3 evidence pointer finding was addressed by binding row_ref to the concrete CP00-166 permission matrix row_id and regenerating the contract projection.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-168 builds frozen permission/audit fixture decision test descriptors only; package code does not load real data, execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, route dispatch, API handlers, UI rendering, AI retrieval, analytics, Hermes, or Claude.

findings.title: permission_matrix_row row_ref resolves to undefined
findings.description: decision_binding_descriptors.permission_matrix_row assigns row_ref from MASTER_DATA_PERMISSION_AUDIT_DECISION_BINDING.permission_matrix_row.row_ref, which evaluates to undefined (confirmed: JSON.stringify drops it, so contract/registry parity only holds because both serialize without row_ref, and the validator's stringify comparison passes). The intended internal evidence pointer never materializes — likely the source property is named differently. No leak, no scope or no-write impact since the field is internal evidence-only and customer surface is unaffected; non-blocking nit worth correcting for evidence completeness.
findings.post_review_disposition: addressed: permission_matrix_row row_ref now points to MASTER_DATA_PERMISSION_AUDIT_DECISION_BINDING.permission_matrix_row.row_id and the regenerated contract mirrors the concrete row_ref value.
